name: BADT Cave
description: Come play pickleball at BADT Cave in Tupelo, MS! There is 1 outdoor court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. Amenities include lights, trainers or lessons, and wheelchair accessibility. The court can be reserved. The court is private.

name: Cotton Mill Pickleball
description: Cotton Mill Pickleball is a place to play pickleball in Tupelo, MS. There are 4 indoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms, water, lights, and trainers or lessons. You can reserve the courts. A one-time fee is required to play.
fees: Fee required — $10 weekday $12 weekendMemberships start at $50/month( Discounts for over 65)

name: Dot Cooper Kelly City Park
description: Come play pickleball at Dot Cooper Kelly City Park in Tupelo, MS! There are 12 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms, lights, and water.

name: East Heights Baptist Church
description: Come play pickleball at East Heights Baptist Church in Tupelo, MS! There are 3 indoor wood courts. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. The courts cannot be reserved. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights.

name: First Baptist Church
description: First Baptist Church is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Tupelo, MS. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are private. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights.

name: First Presbyterian Gym
description: First Presbyterian Gym is a place to play pickleball in Tupelo, MS. There are 3 indoor wood courts. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. The courts are private. Amenities include restrooms and water. The courts cannot be reserved.
open_play_schedule: Seasonal only.  An FPC member must be present.  Park in the back parking lot.

name: First United Methodist Church CAC
description: First United Methodist Church CAC is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Tupelo, MS. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are private.
hours: Monday-Thursday, 9 a.m. - 4 p.m. Plan a Visit Worship Online Experiencing God’s love. Growing in Christ. Serving all people. First United Methodist Church Tupelo is a Lighthouse congregation . All are welcome. name: Milam School Gym
description: Come play pickleball at Milam School Gym in Tupelo, MS! There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. A one-time fee is required to play. You can’t reserve the courts. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water.
open_play_schedule: Available seasonally or during inclement weather.  Must be reserved via Tupelo Pickleball.
fees: Fee required — Free to Tupelo Pickleball members.  $5 drop in fee for non-members.

name: Mill village Tupelo
description: Mill village Tupelo is a place to play pickleball in Tupelo, MS. There are 5 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. Players have access to lights.

name: Spring Lake HOA
description: Come play pickleball at Spring Lake HOA in Tupelo, MS! There are 2 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include lights, restrooms, water, and trainers or lessons. The courts are private. The courts cannot be reserved.

name: Spring Lake Tennis and Pickleball Courts
description: Private courts for residents and their guests only.

name: TCPS COurts
description: these are courts found in the gymnasium of TCPS.  Use is reserved to students of the school when pickleball is being taught in their physical education class. The courts will also be used for a school pickleball club..

name: The Badt Court
description: Come play pickleball at The Badt Court in Tupelo, MS! There is 1 outdoor concrete court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. The court is private. Players have access to trainers or lessons.
